name: Kanagawa Prefecture (Japan)

colors.symbolism: purity, peace and the prefecture's bright future
vitality and the warmth of the prefecture's people

description: A white field charged at the centre with a large red calligraphic abstraction of the kanji 神, the first character in the prefecture's name (神奈川, Kanagawa).
history: Kanagawa's prefectural symbol was selected by public competition and officially adopted on 23 November 1948. The flag, with the red symbol on a white field, has been used as the prefectural flag since the same year.

features.symbolism: a calligraphic abstraction of the first character of 神奈川 (Kanagawa); the stylisation evokes both the harmony of the prefecture and an open, forward-looking dynamism.
